# Using Data Wisely {data-state="goldborder"}

- [Collect]{.alert} the data  
- [Explore]{.alert} the data  
- [Choose]{.alert} a model / procedure  
- [Draw]{.alert} conclusions  

<h3>The data have several roles</h3>

- *Data collection* → what data even exist  
- *Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)* → learn from the data  
- *Model selection* → choose a procedure  
- *Data snooping* 🚫 → reuse data to chase results  

👉 validity starts *before* analysis  

---

## Data collection

- How are data obtained?

- Design matters:

  - random sampling  
  - random assignment  
  - observational vs experimental  

- Issues:

  - bias (systematic error)  
  - confounding (hidden drivers)  
  - missingness (not at random?)  

- Examples:

  - convenience sample → biased estimates  
  - lurking variable → spurious relationship  

👉 No analysis fixes bad data  

---

## Exploratory data analysis (EDA)

:::{.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}

- Look first, decide later  

- Tools:

  - plots (histogram, scatterplot, boxplot)  
  - summaries (mean, median, spread)  
  - transformations (log, scale)  

- What to look for:

  - structure → trends, clusters, nonlinearities  
  - anomalies → outliers, data errors  
  - relationships → associations between variables  

- Examples:

  - skewed data → log transform  
  - funnel shape → non-constant variance  
  - outlier → data error or important case?  

:::

::: {.column width="45%"}


👉 hypothesis-generating, not testing  

::: {.key-point}

"The greatest value of a picture is when it forces us to notice what we never expected"

 — attribued to [John Tukey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Tukey), see his book, 
   *Exploratory Data Analysis* (1977)

:::

:::

:::

## Model selection (broad view)

:::{.columns}

:::{ .column width="50%"}

- Choose among

  - probability models  
  - regression models  
  - test statistics  
  - transformations  
  - distributions / assumptions  

- Criteria:

  - fit (does it match the data?)  
  - simplicity (parsimony)  
  - interpretability  

- Examples:

  - linear vs nonlinear regression  
  - normal vs skewed error model  
  - include/exclude predictors  

:::

:::{ .column width="50%"}
👉 choose a *procedure* for inference  

:::

:::


## Model selection: a subtle point

- Selection uses the data  

- Then we perform inference *as if the model were fixed*  

- Consequence: [uncertainty is underestimated]{.alert}

- Example:

  - try many regressions → report the “best” one  
  - standard errors ignore the search  

👉 $p$-values too small, intervals too narrow  

---

## Data snooping 🚫

- What it is

  - Trying many things on the same data  
  - Reporting what “works”  

- Examples:

  - many hypothesis tests → report significant ones  
  - trying transformations until p < 0.05  

- Consequence: [Type I error is inflated beyond $\alpha$]{.alert}

👉 false discoveries become likely  

---

## Avoiding the data snooping trap

- Separate roles:

  - exploration / training  
  - validation  
  - test  

- Or:

  - pre-specify procedures  
  - use cross-validation  

- Mindset:

  - exploration is flexible  
  - inference must be disciplined  

👉 exploration ≠ confirmation  

---

## Takeaway

- collection → foundation  
- EDA → understand  
- selection → decide  
- snooping → distort  

👉 problems early propagate forward
